ref: When Jesus got up the next morning, he was hungry. He started out for the city,
text: Se mɔtu benɛ e kpale e se nɛ e yaa ma a mi ɔ, hwɔ ye lɛ.
translated: If you are not happy with what I have done, please tell me.

ref: Jesus knew what they were thinking, so he said to them: Any kingdom where people fight each other will end up ruined. And a town or family that fights will soon destroy itself.
text: Se nɛ Yesu na a juɛmi ɔ, e de mɛ ke: “Matsɛ yemi tsuaa matsɛ yemi nɛ gbaa woɔ lɛ nitsɛ e he ɔ, e ywia; nɛ ma tsuaa ma aloo we tsuaa we nɛ gbaa woɔ lɛ nitse e he ɔ, e dɛ si.
translated: If you follow Jesus and do what he says, you will be saved; but if you do not follow him and do what he says, you will be condemned.

ref: You are a bunch of evil snakes, so how can you say anything good? Your words show what is in your hearts.
text: Nyɛ sinɔhi a nina! Kɛ maa pee kɛɛ nɛ nyɛ nɛ nyɛ ji nimli yayamihi ɔ, nyɛ ma nyɛ munyu kpakpa maa tu? Ejakaa nɔ́ nɛ hyi tsui kɛ be nɔ ɔ, lɔ ɔ nɔuu nya deɔ.
translated: Is this the reason you are saying this? If you want to say something, please say it clearly so that we can understand. Don't be afraid to speak your mind.

ref: Everyone will hate you because of me. But if you remain faithful until the end, you will be saved.
text: Nɛ nihi tsuo maa nyɛ nyɛ ngɛ ye biɛ ɔ he; se nɔ nɛ hɛɔ mi kɛ pii si ɔ, lɛ ji nɔ nɛ a ma he e yi wami?
translated: If you don't want to give me anything, then why are you asking me for many things?

ref: They were completely amazed and said, “Everything he does is good! He even heals people who cannot hear or talk.”
text: Nɛ a nya kpɛ a he nitsɛ nitsɛ nɛ a de ke: “E pee nihi kulaa saminya! E ha tuekpokuli nuɔ nɔ, nɛ amuɛtihi hu tuɔ munyu.”
translated: If you see someone who is doing something bad, don't be afraid to speak up. It is better to speak the truth, even if it is difficult.

ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.
text: Jeha nɛ ɔ benɛ Herode na kaa ni leli ɔmɛ sisi lɛ ɔ, e muklii mi fu wawɛɛ, nɛ e tsɔ a ya gbe jokuɛ nyumuwi tsuo nɛ ngɛ Betlehem kɛ lejɛ ɔ ni ɔmɛ a mi tsuo, ninɛmɛ nɛ ye jeha enyɔ kɛ ninɛmɛ nɛ yi ja ngɛ be nɛ e bi ni leli ɔmɛ fitsofitso ɔ nya.
translated: When Herod heard that a king of the Jews had been born in Bethlehem, he was troubled, and all Jerusalem with him.  He called together all the chief priests and scribes of the people and inquired of them where the Christ was to be born.  And they said to him, “In Bethlehem of Judea; for so it is written by the prophet:  ‘And you, Bethlehem, in the land of Judah, are by no means least among the rulers of Judah; for from you shall come a ruler who is to shepherd my people Israel.’”

ref: But I tell you that Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of them.
text: Se i ngɛ nyɛ dee ke: Salmo kɛ e nyami ɔ tsuo tete mia we kaa enɛ ɔmɛ a kpɛti kake.
translated: If you are going to do something, do it well: a good name is better than riches.

ref: But Jesus replied: You want a sign because you are evil and won't believe! But the only sign you will get is the sign of the prophet Jonah.
text: Se Yesu he nɔ nɛ e de mɛ ke: “Yi nɔ bi yayahi kɛ gba puɛli ngɛ okadi hlae; se a be mɛ okadi ko hae, kaa ja gbalɔ Yona okadi ɔ.
translated: Jesus said to them: “Truly, I tell you, it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for someone who is rich to enter the kingdom of God.  Again I tell you, it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for someone who is rich to enter the kingdom of God."

ref: You may be sure a servant who is always faithful will be put in charge of everything the master owns.
text: Niinɛ, i ngɛ nyɛ dee ke, e maa ngɔ lɛ ngɔ to e nihi tsuo kulaa nya.
translated: If you want to do something, you should do it well so that people will praise you.

ref: At noon the sky turned dark and stayed that way until three o'clock.
text: Se kɛ je piani ngmlɛ nyɔngma kɛ enyɔ kɛ ya su ngmlɛ etɛ ɔ, diblii wo ngɛ zugba a tsuo nɔ.
translated: If you want to eat the plantains, you should peel them before you cook them.

ref: But I promise you if you are angry with someone, you will have to stand trial. If you call someone a fool, you will be taken to court. And if you say that someone is worthless, you will be in danger of the fires of hell.
text: Se i ngɛ nyɛ dee ke: nɔ tsuaa nɔ nɛ muklii mi fuɔ e nyɛmi , a kɛ lɛ maa ba kojolɔ ɔ hɛ mi; nɛ nɔ tsuaa nɔ nɛ deɔ e nyɛmi ke: ‘Yaka nɔmlɔ’ ɔ, a kɛ lɛ maa ba gua ngua a hɛ mi; nɛ nɔ tsuaa nɔ nɛ tsɛɛ e nyɛmi ke kuasia nɛ he be senami ɔ, e maa ye abosiami a je la a mi yami fɔ?
translated: If you say it is good: let us go and eat, and then let us go and sleep; and if you say it is bad: let us go and drink, and then let us go and sleep; and if you say it is something else: let us go and do something else, and then let us go and sleep?

ref: On the way they met a man named Simon who was from Cyrene, and they forced him to carry Jesus' cross.
text: Se benɛ a yaa a, a na nyumu Kireneno ko nɛ a tsɛɛ lɛ Simon, nɛ a nyɛ e nɔ ke e wo e sɛumi tso ɔ.
translated: If you see him, tell him that Simon has greeted him and that he should come to me quickly.

ref: Ask the Lord in charge of the harvest to send out workers to bring it in.”
text: Lɔ ɔ he ɔ nyɛ kpa ni kpami nyɔmtsɛ ɔ pɛɛ konɛ e wo ni tsuli blɔ kɛ ba e ni kpami ɔ mi.”
translated: If you are not careful, you will be deceived by the one who is close to you.

ref: They told him, “He will be born in Bethlehem, just as the prophet wrote,
text: Nɛ a de lɛ ke: “Ngɛ Betlehem ngɛ Yudea; se bɔ nɛ gbalɔ ɔ ngma ji nɛ ɔ nɛ:
translated: And it came to pass in those days that a decree went out from Caesar Augustus that all the world should be registered.

ref: But you will not really be the one speaking. The Spirit from your Father will tell you what to say.
text: Ejakaa pi nyɛ ji ninɛmɛ nɛ ngɛ munyu ɔ tue ɔ, se mohu nyɛ tsɛ ɔ mumi ɔ lɛ ngɛ munyu tue ngɛ nyɛ mi.
translated: If you want to eat today, then you should go and fetch it yourself, because I will not fetch it for you.

ref: When Herod found out that the wise men from the east had tricked him, he was very angry. He gave orders for his men to kill all the boys who lived in or near Bethlehem and were two years old and younger. This was based on what he had learned from the wise men.
text: Jeha nɛ ɔ benɛ Herode na kaa ni leli ɔmɛ sisi lɛ ɔ, e muklii mi fu wawɛɛ, nɛ e tsɔ a ya gbe jokuɛ nyumuwi tsuo nɛ ngɛ Betlehem kɛ lejɛ ɔ ni ɔmɛ a mi tsuo, ninɛmɛ nɛ ye jeha enyɔ kɛ ninɛmɛ nɛ yi ja ngɛ be nɛ e bi ni leli ɔmɛ fitsofitso ɔ nya.
translated: When Herod heard that a king of the Jews had been born in Bethlehem, he was troubled, and all Jerusalem with him.  For he had summoned the chief priests and the scribes and inquired of them where the Christ was to be born. And he said to them, "In Bethlehem of Judea, for so it is written by the prophet: 'And you, Bethlehem, in the land of Judah, are by no means least among the rulers of Judah; for from you shall come a ruler who is to shepherd my people Israel.'"

ref: But I tell you that Solomon with all his wealth wasn't as well clothed as one of them.
text: Se i ngɛ nyɛ dee ke: Salmo kɛ e nyami ɔ tsuo tete mia we kaa enɛ ɔmɛ a kpɛti kake.
translated: If you are a good person, then:  Your God will help you in your time of need.

ref: But Jesus replied: You want a sign because you are evil and won't believe! But the only sign you will get is the sign of the prophet Jonah.
text: Se Yesu he nɔ nɛ e de mɛ ke: “Yi nɔ bi yayahi kɛ gba puɛli ngɛ okadi hlae; se a be mɛ okadi ko hae, kaa ja gbalɔ Yona okadi ɔ.
translated: Then Jesus said to them: “You have heard that it was said, ‘You shall not murder; and whoever murders will be liable to judgment.’ But I say to you that everyone who is angry with his brother will be liable to judgment; and whoever says to his brother, ‘Raca,’ will be answerable to the council; and whoever says, ‘You fool,’ will be liable to the fire of hell.

ref: You may be sure a servant who is always faithful will be put in charge of everything the master owns.
text: Niinɛ, i ngɛ nyɛ dee ke, e maa ngɔ lɛ ngɔ to e nihi tsuo kulaa nya.
translated: If you want to eat, you should go and fetch some food for yourself.

ref: At noon the sky turned dark and stayed that way until three o'clock.
